title: Infrared spectroscopy
summary: 9701 AS IR spectroscopy — fingerprint region, functional group absorptions, and interpreting spectra for structure elucidation.
learningObjectives: Explain bond vibration and IR absorption.
Identify key peaks: O–H, C=O, C=C, N–H, C–H.
Use the fingerprint region (below 1500 cm⁻¹) for comparison.
Combine IR data with other analytical techniques.

simpleExplanation.title: Bond vibrations as fingerprints
simpleExplanation.summary: Molecules absorb IR when bond vibration frequency matches radiation energy. Different bonds give characteristic wavenumber ranges.
steps: Sharp ~1710 cm⁻¹: C=O (carbonyl — aldehyde, ketone, acid, ester).
Below 1500 cm⁻¹: fingerprint — unique pattern for comparison.

sections.content: **Infrared spectroscopy** (22.1) is paired with **mass spectrometry** (22.2) in analysis questions. Learn a **table of key peaks** — examiners provide spectra but expect you to assign bonds.

items: Wavenumber units: cm⁻¹ (not wavelength in nm on data sheets)
Broad O–H in acids often overlaps with C–H
C=O shifts: acid ~1710, ester ~1740, amide ~1680 cm⁻¹
Absence of a peak is as informative as presence
